agent_results.claim: Existing YOLO harness boundary audit detects tracked and untracked target-project mutations in git-backed and non-git projects using git ls-files and filesystem walking fallbacks.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() creates SHA-256 digests of all project files using git ls-files for git repos and walkFiles() fallback for non-git repos, excluding allowed artifact roots.
evidence.why: Core boundary detection implementation supporting both git and non-git projects.

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots() compares before/after snapshots to detect added, modified, or deleted files by comparing SHA-256 digests.
evidence.why: Change detection mechanism that identifies all mutations regardless of git tracking status.

evidence.summary: gitFiles() runs 'git ls-files -co --exclude-standard' to capture tracked, cached, and other files including untracked entries.
evidence.why: Git-backed file discovery that includes untracked files in boundary audit.

evidence.summary: walkFiles() provides filesystem walking fallback for non-git repos, reading directory entries recursively while excluding common ignored directories (.git, node_modules, dist).
evidence.why: Non-git project support ensures boundary audit works without git repository.

evidence.summary: Test 'git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations' verifies boundary audit catches untracked files added by agents in git repositories.
evidence.why: Test evidence confirming untracked file detection in git repos.

evidence.summary: Test 'git-backed boundary detects gitignored project mutations outside excluded dirs' verifies boundary detects .gitignore file mutations when they are project files.
evidence.why: Test evidence confirming gitignored files are detected when outside allowed roots.

evidence.summary: Test 'non-git boundary fallback detects modified project files and ignores artifacts' verifies filesystem walking detects mutations while excluding artifact directories from allowed_write_roots.
evidence.why: Test evidence confirming non-git boundary detection with artifact exclusion.

evidence.summary: Boundary violations are converted to BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers and merged into readiness, forcing prd_ready=false when any project mutation is detected.
evidence.why: Blocking enforcement that prevents PRD readiness on boundary violations.

evidence.summary: Real stress test run shows boundary.project_mutation='clean' and changes=[] after full-tool Claude agent execution, confirming no project mutations occurred.
evidence.why: Real-world evidence that boundary audit works correctly with actual Claude agents.

evidence.summary: Demand doctrine states: 'dispatch会在运行前后审计项目文件边界，任何越界改动都会变成readiness blocker' and '边界快照和readiness blocker承担'.
evidence.why: Product documentation confirming boundary audit design intent and enforcement.

assumptions: SHA-256 digests provide sufficient detection for semantic file mutations even when file sizes remain similar.
Git ls-files --exclude-standard adequately covers common ignore patterns while still detecting project mutations.
Filesystem walking fallback provides equivalent coverage for non-git repositories without performance issues.
Transient mutations within the execution window (mutate and revert) would not be detected by snapshot diffing.
Concurrent filesystem changes from other processes during evidence execution could be incorrectly attributed to agents.
risks: Boundary snapshot may produce false positives if temp/cache files are not properly excluded from allowed_write_roots.
Boundary snapshot may have false negatives for large binary files, git submodules, or symlinks that fileDigest() cannot read properly.
Transient file mutations within the execution window (mutate then revert) would escape detection by snapshot diffing.
Concurrent filesystem changes from IDEs, build tools, or other processes during agent execution could be misattributed to agents.

result.notes: Boundary audit implementation is complete with git ls-files for tracked/untracked files, filesystem walking fallback for non-git repos, SHA-256 digest comparison for mutation detection, and blocker enforcement preventing PRD readiness on violations. Tests confirm detection across git untracked, gitignored, and non-git scenarios. Stress tests show clean boundaries with real Claude agents.

agent_results.claim: Existing YOLO harness boundary audit detects tracked and untracked target-project mutations in git-backed and non-git projects using git ls-files and filesystem walking fallbacks.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() implementation verified: creates SHA-256 digests using git ls-files for git repos and walkFiles() fallback for non-git, with allowed artifact root exclusion.
evidence.why: Primary implementation evidence supporting multi-mode boundary detection claim.

evidence.summary: Test suite covers git untracked detection (416-452), gitignored file detection (454-491), and non-git fallback mutation detection (493-533), validating all three claimed detection modes.
evidence.why: Comprehensive test evidence confirming detection capabilities across scenarios.

evidence.summary: Demand doctrine confirms boundary audit design intent and enforcement via readiness blockers.
evidence.why: Product documentation validates architectural intent matches implementation.

evidence.summary: Real stress test artifact shows boundary.project_mutation='clean' after full-tool Claude execution, proving detection works with actual agents.
evidence.why: Real-world evidence confirming theoretical implementation works in practice.

evidence.summary: Blocker enforcement code verified: BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers force prd_ready=false on violations.
evidence.why: Evidence that detection claims are enforced, not just theoretical.

risks: FileDigest() may fail on large binary files, symlinks, or special filesystem objects not tested in current test coverage.
Transient mutations within execution window (mutate then revert before snapshot) escape detection by design.
Concurrent filesystem changes from IDEs, build tools, or background processes during agent execution could cause false positives.
missing: Evidence for large binary file handling (>100MB) in fileDigest() implementation.
Evidence for symlink and special file handling in boundary snapshot.
Evidence for git submodule handling in gitFiles() implementation.
Performance characteristics for very large repositories (100k+ files) not evidenced.

result.notes: Explorer claim is well-supported by code, tests, docs, and stress test artifacts. Evidence paths are valid and readable. Assumptions are properly separated from facts in explorer result. Missing items are edge cases not blocking core claim. Proceed with treating boundary detection claims as project facts for PRD readiness.
